title: Infrastructure Standardization Architecture Plan
description: Six-pronged infrastructure standardization: fd installation, build tool consolidation, test coverage, ADR process, script centralization, dead test wiring
boundary: Monorepo infrastructure tooling — CI/CD, build system, test infrastructure, developer tooling, scripts, documentation process
consumer_purpose: Reduce infrastructure entropy, establish repeatable patterns, and wire existing but disconnected test assets

root_causes.hypothesis: fd is documented in the user's global AGENTS.md as a preferred CLI tool but was never installed locally or added to the repo's development environment (flake.nix, setup scripts, or repo-level AGENTS.md)
evidence: smart_find tool fails with 'fd not found. Install with: brew install fd'
AGENTS.md (repo) does not mention fd or any CLI tool preferences
flake.nix has no fd or ripgrep packages
Multiple sessions show 'fd not available' errors
User's global AGENTS.md at ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md lists fd as preferred tool

root_causes.hypothesis: Build tool diversity is architectural necessity driven by heterogeneous output targets, not accidental fragmentation. Each tool is the idiomatic choice for its target: vite for SolidJS web apps, electron-vite for the Electron desktop shell, astro for static docs, storybook for component catalog, tsc for pure-TypeScript library, bun_build for the CLI core. No two tools serve the same purpose.
evidence: vite (6 packages): app, ui, enterprise, console-app, console-support, stats-app — all SolidJS web apps
electron-vite (1 package): desktop — requires electron-vite for main/preload/renderer bundling
astro (1 package): web — static site generator, cannot be replaced by vite
storybook (1 package): builds a component catalog with storybook-specific tooling
tsc (2 packages): plugin, sdk/js — pure TypeScript libraries needing only declaration emit
bun_build (1 package): opencode — CLI core with complex bundling (migrations, web UI, model data)
root_causes.note: Root cause is NOT tool count. The real problem is absence of a shared build convention layer: no common tsconfig base, no shared vite plugin preset, no build script standardization. Each package independently configures its tooling.

root_causes.hypothesis: The real fragmentation problem is the absence of a shared build convention layer: there is no common TypeScript config base, no shared vite plugin preset, and no `build` script standardization. Every package independently configures its tooling, creating onboarding friction and making cross-package build changes risky.
evidence: turbo.json only registers 'build' as a task with 'dist/**' outputs — no shared config, no env passthrough, no caching strategy beyond defaults
No root-level tsconfig.base.json shared across packages — each package manages its own tsconfig
vite.config.ts files in console/support/ and enterprise/ have no shared base — each independently imports @solidjs/start or tailwind
Only 10 out of ~25 packages have a build script; 11 are source-only consumed via workspace:*
No prebuild/postbuild hooks standardized — desktop has predev/prebuild, others don't

root_causes.hypothesis: Coverage was never set up because bun's coverage support was historically immature, bun test adoption was incremental, and the CI pipeline focused on pass/fail gating rather than quality metrics
evidence: bun test --coverage exists and desktop package has a test:coverage script (proves it works)
No coverage configuration in any CI workflow
turbo.json has no coverage pipeline entries
Root bunfig.toml has no coverage settings
CI focuses on binary pass/fail (bun turbo test:ci) with JUnit XML output only

root_causes.hypothesis: Four packages (enterprise, console-app, stats-core, plugin) have test files that were written but never connected to package.json scripts or CI. The enterprise tests were auto-generated in a 'chore: generate' commit (caf2451da) with no corresponding test script. The plugin test was similarly committed in isolation. These are orphaned artifacts from code generation or scaffolding, not intentionally disconnected.
evidence: enterprise: 2 test files (test/core/storage.test.ts, test/core/share.test.ts) — no 'test' script; auto-generated in commit caf2451da
console-app: 2 test files (test/rateLimiter.test.ts, test/providerUsage.test.ts) — no 'test' script
stats-core: 1 test file (src/domain/inference.test.ts) — colocated test, no 'test' script
plugin: 1 test file (src/__tests__/plugin-transport.test.ts, 24 test assertions) — no 'test' script
console/core: NO test files (surface map incorrectly identified script files as tests — expect() matches from scripts, not tests)
turbo.json only defines test pipelines for opencode, app, and ui — none of the 4 orphaned packages have turbo test entries
All 4 packages have bun:test imports — tests were written for bun but never invoked

root_causes.hypothesis: The docs/adr/ directory was referenced in AGENTS.md as a desired practice but never created. The repo uses docs/json/ for structured artifacts but no ADR schema or template exists, leaving a gap between the stated convention and the actual directory structure.
evidence: AGENTS.md (user global) references 'docs/adr/' for architecture decisions
docs/adr/ does not exist
No ADR schema exists in docs/schemas/
docs/json/ is well-structured but no ADR subdirectory
Build tool choices and test patterns have no documented rationale

root_causes.hypothesis: Scripts accumulated organically in each package's directory as features were added. No convention or justfile existed to centralize cross-cutting scripts, so each package duplicated infrastructure scripting.
evidence: 28 scripts across 4 locations: opencode/script/ (14), desktop/scripts/ (8), sdk/js/script/ (2), app/scripts/ (1)
No root scripts/ directory
No justfile at root
Cross-cutting concerns (publishing, CI validation, setup) span multiple package directories
Several scripts are CI-only (httpapi-exercise.ts, check-migrations.ts) but live in package dirs

root_causes.hypothesis: Environment variables grew without a registry because the Effect Config layered system provides runtime validation, creating a false sense that documentation was unnecessary. The ~100 vars are split across three layers: CI workflow YAML, turbo.json passThroughEnv, and runtime-flags.ts Config definitions, with no single source of truth.
evidence: ~100 env vars, no registry document
9 CI workflow files reference OPENCODE_ vars
turbo.json uses passThroughEnv: ['*'] for test tasks (all vars pass through)
runtime-flags.ts uses Effect Config for structured env var definitions (32 runtime flags)
No single document listing all env vars, their defaults, and their consumers

fixes.title: Install fd and add CLI tool documentation to repo AGENTS.md
fixes.description: Install the fd (fd-find) tool and document all preferred CLI tools in the repository's AGENTS.md. fd is listed in the user's global AGENTS.md (~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md) as a preferred CLI tool but is NOT installed locally and NOT documented in the repo-level AGENTS.md. The smart_find tool depends on fd and currently fails in all agent sessions.
files: AGENTS.md (add 'Development Toolchain' section with CLI tools)
flake.nix (add fd, rg, just to devShell packages)
fixes.before: AGENTS.md (146 lines) has NO CLI tool preferences section. fd not installed — smart_find fails with 'fd not found' on every invocation. flake.nix has no fd, ripgrep, or just packages in devShell.
fixes.after: AGENTS.md includes a 'Development Toolchain' section listing fd, rg, sd, sg, bat, jq, yq, hyperfine, difft, watchexec, just, eza with per-platform install instructions. flake.nix devShell includes fd and ripgrep. smart_find works in all agent sessions.

tests_to_run: nix flake check (if Nix is used)
ci_workflow_changes: none — fd is a local dev tool, not a CI dependency

risks: fd binary name conflict: on some Linux distros, 'fd' is a different package. Use 'fd-find' package name in flake.nix
Nix flake may need lockfile update (nix flake update)
Windows developers using WSL need separate install instructions
validation: which fd → returns path
fd --version → returns version
smart_find in opencode no longer fails with 'fd not found'
nix develop → fd works inside nix shell

fixes.title: Shared build convention layer — tsconfig base, vite preset, build script standardization
fixes.description: Rather than reducing build tool count (each tool serves a distinct purpose), add a shared build convention layer that reduces per-package configuration duplication and onboarding friction. Create a root tsconfig.base.json shared across all packages, a shared vite preset for the 6 vite-based packages, and standardize build scripts across the monorepo. Keep all existing build tools — they are architecturally necessary for their targets.
files: packages/shared-vite-config/ (new package — shared vite preset for SolidJS web apps)
fixes.before: No shared tsconfig base — each of ~25 packages has its own independent tsconfig. No shared vite preset — 6 SolidJS vite configs independently configure solidjs plugins, tailwind, aliases. 10 packages have build scripts but no standardized pattern.
fixes.after: Root tsconfig.base.json with shared compiler settings (moduleResolution: bundler, strict: true, jsx: preserve for solid, paths: {} with workspace mapping). All package tsconfigs extend base. Shared vite preset handles solidjs plugin, tailwind, environment variables, and path aliases. Per-package configs override only what's unique (entry point, output name, env-specific).

tests_to_run: bun turbo typecheck (verify no regressions — all packages still typecheck)
bun turbo build (verify all packages build)
bun turbo test:ci (verify tests pass)
CI: full pipeline on Linux + Windows
ci_workflow_changes: test.yml: existing turbo-based pipeline handles this; no workflow changes needed
typecheck.yml: tsgo handles extends chains natively
breaking_changes: tsconfig extends changes could surface latent type errors if base is stricter than per-package configs
tsgo may resolve @/* paths differently when base tsconfig has paths

risks: tsconfig.base.json must be loose enough to work for all packages — electron (desktop), browser (app), node (opencode), and bundle-less (core). Different `lib` and `types` across packages complicate a shared base.

6 vite configs currently vary — enterprise uses @solidjs/start config, console uses different nitro presets. A shared base must handle 3 distinct vite variants (solidjs/start, vanilla solidjs, nitro).
tsgo (the TypeScript native preview) may handle `extends` chains differently from tsc — test both
Turbo cache: changing tsconfig extends invalidates all typecheck caches
validation: bun turbo typecheck → all packages pass
bun turbo build → all buildable packages succeed
bun turbo test:ci → all tests pass
Diff tsconfig files: verify only 'extends' lines changed, no option drift introduced
Vite dev for app, ui, enterprise → hot reload works
dependencies: fix-E (ADR documenting the convention layer decision)

fixes.title: Test coverage infrastructure with bun --coverage
fixes.description: Add bun test --coverage to CI pipeline with a 0% informational baseline, ramping to 50% over 90 days. Add per-package coverage scripts and a turbo coverage pipeline. Generate Cobertura XML for CI integration.
fixes.before: Zero coverage collection. Desktop package has a test:coverage script but it's not wired to CI. No turbo coverage pipeline. No codecov integration.
fixes.after: All 8 packages with tests have `coverage` and `coverage:ci` scripts. turbo coverage:ci runs all. CI collects Cobertura XML. Baseline 0% threshold with 90-day ramp plan to 50%. Coverage reports uploaded as CI artifacts.

tests_to_run: bun turbo coverage:ci (verify coverage collection works)
bun test --coverage in packages/opencode (verify Cobertura output)
Verify CI artifacts contain coverage XML
ci_workflow_changes: test.yml: add coverage job that runs after unit tests
Coverage job: bun turbo coverage:ci → upload Cobertura XML as artifact
impact.breaking_changes: none — coverage is additive, not blocking

risks: bun test --coverage may be slow for large packages (opencode has ~150 test files)
Cobertura XML format may differ from bun's output — needs format verification
Coverage collection in CI adds build time (estimate: 30-60s per package)
Threshold enforcement at 0% is safe; higher thresholds need per-package tuning
validation: bun test --coverage in packages/opencode → generates coverage report
bun turbo coverage:ci → all packages produce coverage XML
Coverage XML file exists at expected output paths
CI workflow runs without failure (0% threshold is always met)
coverage artifact appears in CI run summary
dependencies: fix-D (dead test wiring — ensures all testable packages have scripts)

fixes.title: Dead test wiring — connect 4 orphaned test packages to CI
fixes.description: Add test, test:ci, and coverage scripts to the 4 packages that have test files but no package.json scripts. Wire them into turbo.json and CI workflows. Enterprise, console-app, stats-core, and plugin have test files that were written but never integrated — enterprise tests were auto-generated (commit caf2451da), plugin test was committed in isolation.
files: turbo.json (add test and test:ci pipeline entries for all 4 packages)
.github/workflows/test.yml (verify these packages' junit XML outputs are collected)

before.enterprise: 2 test files (test/core/storage.test.ts, test/core/share.test.ts); no 'test' script; auto-generated in commit caf2451da

fixes.after: All 4 packages have test, test:ci, and coverage scripts. turbo.json includes test:ci entries for each. CI junit report collects their outputs. Console-app tests depend on console-core build (turbo ^build dependency).

tests_to_run: cd packages/enterprise && bun test (verify tests pass)
cd packages/console/app && bun test (verify tests pass)
cd packages/stats/core && bun test (verify tests pass)
cd packages/plugin && bun test (verify tests pass)
bun turbo test:ci (verify new packages work in turbo pipeline)
ci_workflow_changes: test.yml: verify junit report_paths glob picks up 4 new packages
Console-app tests need console-core build: add turbo ^build dependency
impact.breaking_changes: none — tests are additive, but may fail if tests are stale

risks: Enterprise tests may be stale (auto-generated, never run) — imports may not resolve
Console-app test imports from @opencode-ai/console-core/model.js — needs console-core build output; add turbo ^build dependency
Plugin test uses self-referential import (@opencode-ai/plugin/desktop) — may fail due to workspace resolution edge cases
Stats-core test (inference.test.ts) has co-located test at src/domain/ — unusual pattern but bun test handles it
Enterprise tests write to storage backend — may need CI credentials or mocking
JUnit output path (../../.artifacts/) assumes running from package dir — verify relative path for each package
validation: Step 1: cd packages/enterprise && bun test → tests run (pass or fail — document)
Step 2: cd packages/console/app && bun test → tests run
Step 3: cd packages/stats/core && bun test → tests run
Step 4: cd packages/plugin && bun test → tests run
Step 5: bun turbo test:ci --filter=@opencode-ai/enterprise → works in pipeline
Step 6: bun turbo test:ci → all 4 new packages contribute
Step 7: CI workflow collects junit XML from all 4 new packages

fixes.title: ADR process — JSON-schema-backed template and registry
fixes.description: Create docs/json/adrs/ directory with a JSON-schema-backed ADR template (NOT Markdown — the repo's AGENTS.md documentation policy prohibits new Markdown docs). Define the schema under docs/schemas/. Include an ADR index and AGENTS.md process documentation. Markdown rendering, if needed for human readers, is generated from JSON source via the static renderer.
files: docs/schemas/rig.relay.adr.v1.schema.json (new — JSON schema for structured ADRs)
docs/json/adrs/adr_template.v1.json (new — template with title, status, context, decision, consequences, supersedes, superseded_by)
docs/json/adrs/adr_index.v1.json (new — machine-readable index: status, date, superseded_by links)
AGENTS.md (update: add ADR process — when to write, JSON-first format, docs/json/adrs/ location)
fixes.before: docs/json/adrs/ does not exist. AGENTS.md Documentation Policy mandates canonical JSON artifacts but no ADR schema or template exists. Build tool choices, test strategies, and architectural decisions have no documented rationale.
fixes.after: docs/schemas/rig.relay.adr.v1.schema.json defines the ADR structure (status enum: proposed|accepted|deprecated|superseded, title, context, decision, consequences, supersedes, superseded_by, date). docs/json/adrs/ contains the template and index. AGENTS.md documents the ADR workflow. Static renderer generates human-readable Markdown from JSON source.

tests_to_run: uv run python scripts/rig_relay_validate_schemas.py (schema validation)
diff: verify index references both ADRs

risks: First ADRs in the monorepo — establish the pattern that all future decisions follow
Static renderer must be updated to handle ADR rendering from JSON (or use existing rig.documentation.page.v1 schema)
validation: docs/schemas/rig.relay.adr.v1.schema.json validates as JSON Schema
docs/json/adrs/adr_template.v1.json validates against the schema
docs/json/adrs/adr_index.v1.json is valid JSON with correct links
AGENTS.md references docs/json/adrs/ and the ADR workflow
scripts/render_static_docs.py produces readable ADR pages (optional)

fixes.title: Script organization — justfile for developer UX + cross-cutting CI scripts
fixes.description: Add a root justfile with common recipes (build, test, typecheck, lint, dev, coverage). Consolidate cross-cutting operational scripts within the existing root script/ directory (NOT create a conflicting scripts/ directory — root script/ already exists with 15+ files). Add just to the toolchain. Package-specific scripts stay where they are — script dispersal is intentional monorepo design (foundation subagent confirmed this is correct).
files: justfile (new — root, thin wrappers around npm scripts)
script/ (existing — reorganize subdirectories: ci/, dev/, publish/)
justfile_recipes: setup → just clean && bun install && just build
fixes.before: No justfile. Root script/ directory exists (15+ operational scripts: publish.ts, generate.ts, format.ts, beta.ts, changelog.ts, etc.) but no developer-facing recipe runner. Developers discover commands through package.json scripts or CI workflow files.
fixes.after: Root justfile with 12 recipes — all thin wrappers around existing npm/turbo commands. just installed via brew/nix. AGENTS.md documents just as the entry point for common tasks. Root script/ gains ci/, dev/, publish/ subdirectories for better organization.

tests_to_run: just typecheck → passes
just test → passes
just build → passes
just --list → shows all recipes
bun run script/publish.ts (verify still works after reorganization)
ci_workflow_changes: None required — CI workflows reference script/* paths which don't change
Optional: CI could use `just test` but not necessary
breaking_changes: just must be installed by all developers
Root script/ reorganization must not break CI workflow references to script/*.ts files

risks: Root script/ ALREADY EXISTS (15+ files) — must consolidate INTO it, not create conflicting scripts/ directory
publish.yml (492 lines) references ./script/publish.ts — any reorganization must not break publish workflow
script/hooks/ directory has husky git hooks — do NOT move or rename
justfile creates a third task runner (alongside npm scripts and turbo.json) — risk of drift between just recipes and npm equivalents
justfile recipes must be thin wrappers: `test: bun turbo test:ci`. Do NOT duplicate logic.
Nix users get just via flake.nix — homebrew users use `brew install just` — document both
validation: just --list → shows all recipes
just typecheck → same result as bun turbo typecheck
just test → same result as bun turbo test:ci
CI workflows pass with unchanged script/ references
bun run script/publish.ts → still works (path unchanged)
script/hooks/ → husky git hooks still function
dependencies: fix-A (just is a CLI tool needing install documentation)

fixes.title: Environment variable registry
fixes.description: Create a centralized environment variable registry as a structured JSON document documenting all ~100 env vars: name, purpose, default, consumers, and whether it's a CI secret or local config. This is documentation-only — no code changes.
fixes.before: ~100 env vars scattered across 9 CI workflow files, turbo.json, runtime-flags.ts, and source code. No single document lists them all.
fixes.after: Single registry document with all env vars categorized by domain (CI secrets, runtime flags, build vars, deployment vars). Schema validates structure.

tests_to_run: JSON schema validation

risks: Registry may become stale if not kept in sync with code
CI secrets should NOT be documented with values, only names and purposes
validation: Schema validates
All OPENCODE_ env vars from CI workflows are listed
All runtime-flags.ts Config entries are listed
CI secrets are listed by name only (no values)

apply_order: fix-A (fd install + AGENTS.md CLI tools — immediate quick win, no dependencies)
fix-D (dead test wiring — 4 orphaned test packages, validate tests pass)
fix-B (shared build convention layer — tsconfig base + vite preset, Phase 2)
fix-C (test coverage — bun --coverage + CI gate, requires dead test wiring complete)
fix-F (justfile developer UX + root script/ organization — depends on fd/just installed)
fix-G (env var registry — documentation, can be done anytime)

phase_1_immediate.description: Quick wins that establish infrastructure foundations. Ship within 1 week.
gates: fd installed and documented in AGENTS.md
4 dead test packages have scripts and tests pass locally
bun turbo test:ci still passes with new packages

phase_2_conventions.description: Shared build conventions and coverage infrastructure. Ship within 3 weeks.
gates: ADR 0001 documents shared build convention layer decision
tsconfig.base.json works for all packages (tsgo --noEmit passes)
Shared vite preset doesn't break any of the 6 vite-based packages
Coverage reports generated in CI for all test-bearing packages

phase_3_developer_experience.description: Developer tooling and documentation. Ship within 4 weeks.
gates: justfile recipes all work and are thin wrappers around npm scripts
Root script/ organization is backward-compatible with existing CI references
Env var registry is comprehensive and schema-validated

rollback_plan.strategy: Each fix is independently reversible. Revert in reverse order of application.

per_fix.fix-A: Remove fd from flake.nix devShell, revert AGENTS.md changes
per_fix.fix-E: Delete docs/adr/ directory, revert AGENTS.md (no code impact)
per_fix.fix-D: Remove test scripts from 3 package.json files, revert turbo.json additions
per_fix.fix-B: Revert plugin and sdk/js package.json to tsc builds, delete vite.config.ts files
per_fix.fix-C: Remove coverage scripts, revert turbo.json coverage pipeline, revert CI workflow coverage job
per_fix.fix-F: Move scripts back to package directories, delete root scripts/, delete justfile, revert CI paths
per_fix.fix-G: Delete env_var_registry.v1.json and schema (no code impact)

fix-A.note: AGENTS.md in this repo has NO CLI tool section — this fix CREATES the convention. The user's global AGENTS.md at ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md lists preferred tools but the repo-level instruction file (146 lines) does not. Adding a 'Development Toolchain' section is a new convention. flake.nix already defines devShell packages — adding fd/just follows that existing pattern.

fix-E.note: Using JSON-schema-backed format in docs/json/adrs/ follows the repo's Documentation Policy: 'New project documentation and persisted mission outputs must be written as canonical JSON artifacts.' Schema under docs/schemas/ matches existing 15 schemas. Static renderer generates Markdown from JSON source — consistent with docs/json/ → docs/pages/ pipeline.

fix-D.note: Adding scripts to package.json follows the existing pattern. turbo.json pipeline entries match existing test:ci patterns exactly.

fix-B.note: 6 packages already use vite — migrating 2 more aligns with the dominant pattern. Vite config files follow the existing pattern in app, ui, enterprise, etc.

fix-C.note: Desktop package already has a test:coverage script. Adding coverage scripts to other packages standardizes this pattern.

fix-F.note: No root scripts/ directory precedent. No justfile exists anywhere in the repo. Root script/ already exists (singular, 15+ operational scripts) — this fix adds justfile and reorganizes within the existing directory. turbo.json and root package.json scripts are the existing orchestration surface. Justfile adds a third task runner — risk of drift. Recommended: justfile as thin wrappers only, no script migration from packages.

out_of_scope: dependabot.yml — documented as missing but not in scope for this plan (separate slice)
Typecheck workflow improvement (no caching, no matrix) — documented but not in scope
Nix flake comprehensive dev tooling beyond fd and rg — future slice
Test directory pattern standardization (test/, __tests__/, colocated) — separate consistency slice
CI workflow consolidation (27 workflows) — separate optimization slice
Script language standardization (TypeScript + bun vs Node.js) — separate slice
Turbo remote caching setup — future slice
Console/core tests — surface map was wrong; this package has no test files

recorded_findings.finding: Surface map incorrectly reports 4 packages with dead tests. console/core has no test files — the 'expect(' matches came from script files (lookup-user.ts, credit-workspace.ts, billing.ts) not test files. Actual count is 3 packages.

Repo AGENTS.md has no CLI tool preferences section. The user's global AGENTS.md at ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md lists preferred tools (rg, fd, sd, sg, bat, jq, yq, hyperfine, difft, watchexec, just, eza) but the repo-level instruction file does not.

Turbo test pipelines use 'passThroughEnv: ["*"]' for test tasks, passing all ~100 env vars to every test. This is overly broad and makes env var dependencies invisible. Should be narrowed to specific vars.
